summary.narrative: West Houston Corporate Housing Platform comprises two components: (i) the GoWell Americas LLC corporate office at 5050 Westway Park Blvd, Suite 100, Houston TX 77041 — a third-party office lease held by the operating company (not Zhang-owned); and (ii) an 80-unit employee housing complex at 1720 Eldridge Pkwy, Houston TX, owned by Zhang West Houston Housing LLC (Zhang Xi / Zhang Family Trust 100%) and master-leased to GoWell Americas LLC under an arm's-length corporate housing agreement. Zhang-controlled PropCo owns the real estate; GoWell OpCo pays a corporate housing master lease and provides the accommodation to rotating field engineers and technical staff. The most time-critical issue is the Regions Bank senior loan ($3.65M, 5.15% fixed) maturing 15 August 2026 — lender engagement must begin by June 2026. A seller note ($450K, 8.25%) matures February 2027, creating a compacted maturity wall.
summary.keyMessage: CRITICAL: Regions Bank $3.65M matures 15 Aug 2026 + Seller Note $450K matures Feb 2027 — refinancing package must launch June 2026 · 65/80 units occupied · GoWell master lease below-market ($450 vs $850–950 market) — DSCR risk on refinancing

basicInfo.assetType: Employee Housing Complex — PropCo / OpCo Corporate Master Lease
basicInfo.location: 1720 Eldridge Pkwy, Houston TX 77077 (housing complex, Zhang-owned) | 5050 Westway Park Blvd, Suite 100, Houston TX 77041 (operating office, GoWell-leased)
basicInfo.currentStatus: Master Lease — 65/80 units occupied, GoWell Americas LLC as master lessee
basicInfo.ownershipSummary: Zhang West Houston Housing LLC (Zhang Xi / Zhang Family Trust 100%); GoWell Americas LLC as corporate housing master lessee — related-party lease requiring SEC/auditor disclosure
basicInfo.tenure: Housing complex: Freehold (Zhang-owned LLC) | Office 5050 Westway: Operating lease (GoWell OpCo)
basicInfo.masterLeaseTerms: GoWell Americas LLC master lease: $29,250/month ($350/unit × 65 occupied + $350/unit × 15 vacant — minimum occupancy guarantee); employees sub-pay $450/month through payroll deduction; GoWell absorbs $-100 subsidy per occupied unit; lease renews annually with 90-day notice

actions.title: Regions Bank Facility Refinancing — URGENT, Loan Matures 15 Aug 2026
actions.recommendation: Initiate formal refinancing process immediately. Contact Regions Bank for extension/rollover terms; simultaneously approach 2–3 alternative lenders (Wells Fargo Real Estate, CBRE Capital Markets, Berkadia). Target: 5-year term, sub-5.50% fixed rate, interest-only period to manage cashflow. If refinancing cannot be secured before August 15, explore bridge financing (6 months) to buy time.
actions.decisionNeeded: Authorise GoWell US Inc. CFO to execute refinancing mandate — board resolution required

actions.title: HVAC Replacement — Building A, West Wing (Phase 2)
actions.recommendation: Building A west wing HVAC units (installed 1997) have exceeded typical 25-year useful life. Phase 1 (east wing) was completed Q4 2024. Phase 2 scope covers 24 units; estimated USD 185,000. Contractor quotations to be obtained before August 2026.
actions.decisionNeeded: Approve Phase 2 CAPEX budget of USD 185,000

actions.title: Office Lease Renewal — 5050 Westway Park Blvd (expires Oct 2027)
actions.recommendation: Begin landlord discussions 12–18 months before lease expiry. The Energy Corridor market has softened (~15% vacancy); GoWell should leverage to negotiate a renewal at 5–8% below current rent or seek concessions (rent-free period, fit-out allowance). Review space requirements given potential headcount growth to 80+ post-listing.
actions.decisionNeeded: Confirm whether to renew, downsize, or relocate — headcount forecast required

topRisks.title: Loan Maturity Default Risk (Aug 2026)
topRisks.impact: Failure to refinance USD 4.1M by 15 Aug 2026 triggers default; potential cross-default on GoWell corporate facilities and U.S. U.S. listing disruption.

topRisks.title: Houston Commercial RE Market Softness
topRisks.impact: Energy Corridor vacancy at ~18%; subsidised dorm use limits appraised income — lenders may require lower LTV in refinancing, forcing equity injection.

topRisks.title: HVAC Capital Expenditure Exposure
topRisks.impact: Deferred Phase 2 HVAC replacement increases risk of unit non-habitability and tenant safety complaints during summer months.

marketSnapshot.submarket: Energy Corridor, West Houston — Multi-Unit Residential / Commercial
marketSnapshot.assetVsMarket: Dormitory subsidised at $450/mo vs. market $950/mo — significant below-market occupancy cost for employees
